name: Western Union
description_short: At 525 El Camino Real, Millbrae, CA 94030, this Western Union location provides check cashing and money transfer services. Call +1 650-652-3416 for more information.
description_long:
Western Union was founded in 1851 as the New York and Mississippi Valley Printing Telegraph Company in Rochester, New York, and evolved into a global financial services corporation headquartered in Denver, Colorado. Originally dominant in the American telegraphy industry from the 1860s to 1980s, the company pioneered wire money transfer services beginning in 1871 and gradually transitioned away from communications. The company ceased all communications operations in 2006 and is now recognized as the world's largest money-transfer business, serving a global customer base.

Western Union provides a comprehensive suite of financial services including wire transfers, money orders, money transfers, bill payment solutions, transfer tracking, and price estimation tools. As a publicly traded company on the NYSE (ticker: WU), it operates with significant financial resources and global infrastructure, generating $4.36 billion in revenue as of 2023. The company maintains 9,100 employees as of December 2024 and serves customers worldwide through an extensive network of locations and digital platforms.

What distinguishes Western Union is its 170+ year legacy, established reputation, and market-leading position in international money transfers. The company is particularly recognized for serving immigrant communities sending money home, a demographic that The New York Times highlighted as central to the company's sustained business model. Its global reach, brand recognition, and integration into both physical locations and digital platforms provide customers with multiple access points for financial transactions.

Western Union's business model centers on financial services rather than lending or credit products. Customers should be aware that while the company offers convenience and global reach, it operates as a transaction-based service provider rather than a credit-building or financial counseling entity. The company's profitability ($626 million net income in 2023) demonstrates operational stability, making it a reliable option for those needing money transfer and payment services.
pros: Global reach with worldwide money transfer capabilities and established international infrastructure
Market leader in money transfers with 170+ years of brand recognition and trust
Multiple service offerings including wire transfers, money orders, bill payment, and transfer tracking
Publicly traded company (NYSE: WU) with substantial financial resources and operational stability
Serves immigrant communities specifically with reliable remittance services
Digital and physical location access points for customer convenience
Established regulatory compliance and operational track record across international markets
cons: Transaction fees may be higher compared to some digital-only money transfer competitors
No credit-building, lending, or financial counseling services offered
Legacy infrastructure may not be as technologically advanced as newer fintech competitors
Focus on money transfer limits utility for customers seeking broader financial services
Not suitable for consumers seeking credit repair, personal loans, or debt management solutions
best_for: Immigrants and expatriates sending money to family members in home countries
International business professionals requiring reliable wire transfer services
Customers needing bill payment services with established payment processing
Users who value brand recognition and 170+ year operational history over emerging alternatives
diagnosis: Western Union is best for customers—particularly immigrants and international users—who need reliable, globally-accessible wire transfer and money order services with established brand credibility. The primary caveat is that Western Union is a transaction service provider, not a lending or credit-building institution, making it unsuitable for consumers seeking loans, credit repair, or financial counseling.
